claim_short: 1605.04900 Krulisova C*-algebras have a quantitative version of Pelczynski's property (V) Section 5 asks whether the quantitative Grothendieck property wck_{X'}({x_n'}) <= c delta_{w*}(x_n') implies Bendova's c-Grothendieck inequality delta(x_n') <= C delta_{w*}(x_n') with a larger constant. Full affirmative answer: for every Banach space X and bounded sequence in X', delta(x_n') <= delta_{w*}(x_n') + 4...
notes: registry_status=full | review_focus=Review should check the constant in the elementary annihilator lemma: if h in X''' annihilates the canonical copy of X, then ||h|| <= 2 dist(h, X'). The rest of the proof is a direct cluster-point argument. The packet does not claim to solve the separate...
packet_title: Full Result: Range-Weak-Compactness Quantification Implies Bendova Grothendieck
source_question: Source paper: Hana Krulisova, *C*-algebras have a quantitative version of Pelczynski's property (V)*, arXiv:1605.04900. Location: page 9, Section 5, after the displayed inequality involving wck_{X'}({x_n' : n in N}). Question: whether the quantitative Grothendieck property wck_{X'}({x_n'}) <= c delta_{w*}(x_n') implies Bendova's c-Grothendieck inequality delta(x_n') <= C delta_{w*}(x_n') for a larger constant.
source_title: C*-algebras have a quantitative version of Pelczynski's property (V)
